perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video starts in a warehouse-style retail environment with high ceilings and industrial shelving. A woman wearing a black beret and a purple off-the-shoulder top is holding a large, plush Judy Hopps head cushion. A second woman stands slightly behind her holding a Nick Wilde cushion. They are smiling and gesturing towards the items. (2) The camera moves closer to the plushies as the main character points to the facial features, including the large purple eyes and pink nose, demonstrating the texture and 3D construction. Price tags are visible on the shelves in the background. (3) The women demonstrate a functional feature where they can slide their hands into the back or ears of the cushions. Finally, the main woman stacks both cushions and then holds the Judy Hopps cushion over her head, obscuring her face momentarily before the video ends.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Fast-paced, upbeat pop-style background music begins immediately. A high-pitched, energetic female voice speaks in Mandarin, sounding very excited and enthusiastic. (2) The music continues at a steady volume as the primary speaker describes the product details, mentioning prices and comparing them to Disney store prices. A second female voice is heard briefly in the background, sounding supportive and equally excited. (3) The audio remains consistent with the upbeat BGM and the main speaker's rapid, friendly narration until the end, concluding with a lighthearted suggestion for Halloween usage.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) A large 'COSTCO WHOLESALE' logo is fixed at the top of the screen. Decorative yellow and red text appears: '超巨大' (Super Huge) and a hook '你的行李箱裝得下這麽大的嗎？' (Can your suitcase fit something this big?). (2) Functional subtitles appear at the bottom translating the spoken dialogue into Traditional Chinese. High-contrast hook text says '好市多超便宜' (Costco is super cheap) and '動物方城市抱枕' (Zootopia pillows). (3) A decorative text box with a star icon appears saying '我可以幫它掏耳朵' (I can clean its ears) to highlight a specific feature. The overall tone is energetic and promotional.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro Hook -> Product Detail Showcase -> Feature Demonstration -> Outro/Usage Suggestion

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator in focus, 1 female friend/assistant helping hold plushies.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman wearing a black Kangol beret, a purple off-the-shoulder ruffled top, and a black lace skirt.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A typical Costco warehouse interior with tall industrial racks, bedding products on shelves, price tags, and other shoppers visible in the distance.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Price/Product Comparison -> Quality Close-up -> Function Demo -> Conclusion
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a lifestyle shopping recommendation aimed at Disney fans. By showcasing the size and perceived high quality of Zootopia plush cushions at a significantly lower price point (645 NTD) than official Disney parks, the creator encourages viewers to visit Costco for a 'bargain' find. The high energy and physical demonstration emphasize the product's value and 'cuteness' factor.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The presence of a large permanent Costco logo and the specific pricing/product highlight suggest a retail recommendation or integrated store promotion.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: High-energy Mandarin narration, heavy use of variety-show style on-screen text, and 'hidden gem' shopping theme.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively uses visual scale (holding the huge plush heads) to create a visual hook. The comparison between Costco prices and Disney theme park prices is a key psychological trigger for the target audience.
